items.title: Government moves on AI data centres and copyright

children.content: The prime minister has unveiled Labor's plan for new AI national standards, warning Australia has a narrow window to set AI's "social licence".
A new set of standards for artificial intelligence will provide clear rules for data centres, forcing them to minimise water usage and underwrite or supply their own power.
Anthony Albanese has vowed AI companies will not be able to use Australian books, music, art or news to train AI "without the artist's control".

gallery.altText: image of black building with water tanks and infrastructure

thumbnail.altText: image of black building with water tanks and infrastructure

items.title: GP clinic network hit by major cyber breach

children.content: Partnered Health, which operates more than 50 general practice and skin cancer clinics across Australia, has identified more than a dozen clinics where it fears patient information was taken.

image.altText: A white front reception desk of a medical clinic, with a white business sign, and a TV and open door in one corner.

items.title: Reserves that prevented 'full-blown' oil shock running low, IMF says

children.content: The International Monetary Fund says the global economy was spared the worst of the oil price shock because of large reserves. Now they are running out.

image.altText: Rows of white oil tanks are seen outdoors in front of a refinery.

items.title: Trump beats his own record for strategic backflips on Iran

children.content: Donald Trump's actions this week have only confirmed he has run out of options and is now engaged in a simple war of wills, and calculations on who will run out of munitions first, with an enemy that he has underestimated from the start.

gallery.altText: A close-up of a man with blonde hair and a navy suit

thumbnail.altText: A close-up of a man with blonde hair and a navy suit

items.title: US launches strikes on key Iranian island amid renewed threats

children.content: US Central Command said it launched a 90-minute attack targeting Iran's strategic Greater Tunb Island.

image.altText: Black and white aerial view of explosion.

items.title: Sydney uni chief regrets handling of pro-Palestinian encampments

children.content: A royal commission hears how both the universities of Melbourne and Sydney have improved processes for students to make complaints in the wake of antisemitic experiences by staff and students, which escalated in 2024.

image.altText: Mark Scott wearing a suit and tie and glasses sitting at a desk looking forward

items.title: The Odyssey may be the epic we deserve, but not the one we need

children.content: The scope of Christopher Nolan's latest epic might be awesome but a menagerie of supporting faces and questionable characterisations muddle the director's vision.

gallery.altText: A man dressed as a solider stands in front of a crowd and ships.
A beach with soilders and a giant wooden horse

Penelope (Anne Hathaway) waits for years for Odyssus (Matt Damon) to return from war.

items.title: Disgraced footballer Nicky Winmar removed from AFL Hall of Fame

children.content: Winmar was found guilty earlier this month of three counts of unlawful assault and common law assault.

image.altText: Nicky wearing a grey coat and sunglasses.

items.title: An alias and a dead man's name: Former ATO executive charged with fraud

children.content: Former assistant tax commissioner Nick Petroulias has been charged with multiple fraud offences, accused of obtaining financial advantage by deception and using false documents.

image.altText: A man's profiled outside a brick building.

items.title: Trump reverses temporary pause on traffic stops after recent fatal shootings

children.content: US President Donald Trump says federal immigration agents will continue carrying out vehicle stops, reversing a temporary pause announced by officials just a day earlier.

image.altText: A protester holds a sign up in their right hand which reads, Ice Not Welcome Here.

items.title: Diarrhoea-inducing Cyclospora outbreak spreads across US

children.content: The US CDC is investigating thousands of possible cases across 34 states, as officials track down the source of the outbreak.

image.altText: A lot of small lettuce plants growing in a green house.

items.title: 'They hate us and we hate them': England-Argentina semi will come with extra feeling

children.content: It is one of the biggest rivalries in men's World Cup history, and while the teams may downplay the history, England and Argentina fans are ready for another iconic showdown.

articles.title: Why the England-Argentina rivalry is one of the ugliest in football

children.content: England's World Cup semifinal clash against Argentina is the latest chapter in a sporting rivalry that dates back 60 years to an ill-tempered quarterfinal, featuring cheating, violence and redemption.

image.altText: Diego Maradona screams

articles.title: France falters at first real hurdle as superstars fail to shine

children.content: France's 2026 World Cup campaign is ended by a poor 2-0 defeat to Spain, as the much-hyped collection of star forwards fail to turn up for the biggest game yet.

image.altText: Desire Doue pulls his jersey over his face in disappointment

England fans have arms outstretched and sing exuberantly in a pub

items.title: Moira Deeming drops court bid against party after last-ditch plea to retain candidacy

children.content: Victorian MP Moira Deeming faces being disendorsed as a Liberal candidate for refusing to apologise to former party leader Matthew Guy, after accusing him of assaulting her at a community event in May.

image.altText: Moira Deeming walking with a folder in her hand.

items.title: School worker on child abuse material charges denied bail

children.content: The 35-year-old woman, who is seven months' pregnant, is in custody after police discovered alleged child abuse material on her mobile phone.

image.altText: Woman smiling

items.title: The bittersweet reality of becoming an empty-nester

children.content: Experts say there can be unexpected grief for parents that comes from children leaving home.

items.title: Monet, Renoir, van Gogh masterpieces appear in two new blockbuster exhibitions

children.content: More than 150 years after the first Impressionist exhibition in Paris, the Art Gallery of South Australia and Geelong Gallery cast new light on the influential art movement.

image.altText: Three paintings hung in a gallery with red walls.

items.title: Quiz: Dust off your passport and join us on a trip

children.content: Which of these peaks isn't in the Himalayas? And in what country would you find the island of Rapa Nui?

image.altText: Four Easter Island Moai silhouettes in a single file line on a platform in front of an orange setting sun and cloudy sky

items.title: Ex-Sydney church turned LGBT nightclub ordered to 'cease offensive trading'

children.content: The LGBTQIA+ venue said it was exploring its legal options after being issued with a breach of lease notice just days after opening at a former Sydney church.

items.title: Wife recounts horror as husband sucked out of Ryanair plane window

children.content: A woman describes hearing a bang before seeing her husband almost get sucked into space during a flight from Greece to Germany.

image.altText: A smashed plane window.

items.title: One-way 'speedboats with explosives' launched by US for first time

children.content: The United States is using one-way drone boats in combat against Iran for the first time in its military history. A defence expert says America is catching on to this "cheaper and more effective" tactic employed by Iran and Ukraine.

image.altText: The Corsair drone boat appears to be turning in the sea.

items.title: Dog walkers turn tables on would-be sex attacker, police say

children.content: Police are investigating after two women reported that a man attempted to assault them before the dogs they were walking chased him away.

gallery.altText: Dogs chasing a man along a creek

thumbnail.altText: Dogs chasing a man along a creek

items.title: Sydney childcare abuse investigation expands to South Australia

children.content: A federal police investigation into an alleged childcare abuser has expanded to a remote Indigenous community in South Australia.

image.altText: Cloise up of man grinning in a green shirt, plant behind him

items.title: Man shot dead in Sydney's north-west in 'very brazen attack'

children.content: A man has been shot dead in Sydney's north-west, with the homicide squad now leading the investigation.

gallery.altText: Three officers, police tape and a white car at the crime scene.

thumbnail.altText: Three officers, police tape and a white car at the crime scene.

items.title: Australia's wine grape crush plummets to lowest in more than 25 years

children.content: Australian wine grape production has fallen to its lowest amount in more than 25 years as the industry adjusts to a combination of depressed market conditions.

image.altText: A farmer in green shirt leans on the end of a winegrape vineyard holding his hat

items.title: Australian lamb the focus of US trade commission review

children.content: The United States International Trade Commission is investigating sheep meat imports, including from Australia, as American farmers struggle.

image.altText: Close up image of lamb and other meat in a cabinet at a butcher

items.title: Plumber takes down alleged car thief in viral video, but lawyer warns of risk

children.content: A Perth plumber has gone viral on social media after chasing down and restraining an alleged car thief after a botched getaway, but a prominent lawyer warns citizens' arrests are risky.

thumbnail.altText: A man with someone in a headlock

items.title: Truck driver's sacking for $100k ice cream bungle was fair, finds commission

children.content: The Fair Work Commission rules a trucking company was within its rights to fire a driver after he set the temperature of his trailer full of ice cream more than 20 degrees too warm.

image.altText: A road train with the logo EHR in green.

items.title: Broncos losing streak makes finals hopes virtually an impossible task

children.content: Brisbane must reach 13 wins to have any chance of playing finals in 2026, but even that wasn't enough for them in 2022 and may not be again this year.

image.altText: A group of Brisbane Broncos players stand in a line looking dejected after an opposition try.

Tyrannosaurus rex
skeletons discovered makes history as the most valuable dinosaur fossil bought at auction.
